bringing them together for a dinner and or a small group type seminar where we taught them some of the systems and then of course mentioned the exit strategy and different things like that but you said pushing high-class people like that to a room is a bad idea. Well no I didn't say that. No no no not at all. I said that the difficulty of putting them in the room goes up based on two factors. How much money they're leaving, they're giving up from chair side value for the days they're out of the office and the ego problem of being in the room. So it's much harder for example to get heart surgeons in a room than it is podiatrists. The podiatrists don't have a big ego issue and a day out of the office is not that big of money. A guy who's not cutting on hearts, it's big money and he's got a giant ego. That doesn't even mean you wouldn't try under certain circumstances to drag their asses into a room. I'm not saying it's a bad idea and there's almost no substitute for selling in a physical controlled environment that is anywhere near as good. So I mean from for five years I mean we put Kairos and Dennis in rooms all across the country. Evenings. And easier to get to Kairos in the room than the Dennis but not at all impossible to get the Dennis in the room and generally speaking those two niches are seminar goers so it's you know they're somewhat conditioned. Now it's harder to get the cosmetic Dennis in the room than it is the regular Dennis in the room for because both factors start to apply. A the cost of him being away is greater and his ego is greater. Right so and there's fewer of them to pull from. So however many Dennis there are in Cleveland there's a much smaller number of cosmetic Dennis in Cleveland.
